name: Render Pick CHOP
description: The Render Pick CHOP samples a rendering (from a Render TOP or a Render Pass TOP) and returns 3D information from the geometry at that particular pick location. Values sampled can include position, normals, point color, texture coordinates, depth and the object's path.	An Info DAT should be used to retrieve the object's path.
summary: The Render Pick CHOP samples a rendering (from a Render TOP or a Render Pass TOP) and returns 3D information from the geometry at that particular pick location. Values sampled can include position, no

parameters.name: Strategy
parameters.label: Strategy
parameters.description: Decides when to update values based on pick interactions.

parameters.name: While Select
parameters.label: While Select
parameters.description: select - holdfirst - Holds the values first returned when geometry picked. holdlast - Holds the values last returned when geometry picked. This differs from the Continuous strategy in that it will hold the last values picked on geometry if the pick starts sampling empty space (no geometry in that part of the scene). Alternatively, using the Continuous strategy the values will be cleared to zero if the pick starts sampling empty space. always -

parameters.name: Clear Previous Pick on New Pick
parameters.label: Clear Previous Pick on New Pick
parameters.description: clearprev - This parameter is only enabled when the Strategy is set to Hold Last Picked. When this is on, starting a new pick on empty space will clear the values. When off, the last values will be held if the pick starts on empty space.

parameters.name: Response Time
parameters.label: Response Time
parameters.description:
responsetime -  - Determines when the values are updated.

 nextcook - The values are captured on the current frame and updated next frame. Results are from the previous frame, but much faster cook times. thiscook - The values are captured and updated in the current frame.

parameters.name: Next Cook (Faster)
parameters.label: Next Cook (Faster)
parameters.description: nextcook - The values are captured on the current frame and updated next frame. Results are from the previous frame, but much faster cook times. thiscook - The values are captured and updated in the current frame.

parameters.name: Pick Radius
parameters.label: Pick Radius
parameters.description: pickradius - Controls the radius of the search area for the pick. If nothing is found at the pick's center it will keep searching for geometry in the search area defined by the Pick Radius.

parameters.name: Pick Radial Step
parameters.label: Pick Radial Step
parameters.description: pickradstep - Used to reduce the searching within the search area. The search area is sampled at locations that correspond to 'spokes' outwards from the center pick point.

parameters.name: Pick Circular Step
parameters.label: Pick Circular Step
parameters.description: pickcirstep - Used to reduce the searching within the search area. The search area is sampled at locations that correspond to 'rings' outwards from the center pick point.

parameters.name: Render/RenderPass TOP
parameters.label: Render/RenderPass TOP
parameters.description: rendertop - Specifies which render to sample.

parameters.name: Use Pickable Flags
parameters.label: Use Pickable Flags
parameters.description: usepickableflags - When turned on only geometry whose Pickable Flag is on can be selected by the Render Pick . The Pickable Flag is found on all Object components.

parameters.name: Include Non-Pickable Objects
parameters.label: Include Non-Pickable Objects
parameters.description: includenonpickable - Includes the non-pickable objects in the picking algorithm such that non-pickable objects may occlude pickable objects.  For example, if there is only one pickable object in the scene with lots of additional non-pickable geometry is present, turning this parameter on will prevent the pickable object from being selected if it is behind a non-pickage object (occluded by the non-pickage object).

parameters.name: Picking by
parameters.label: Picking by
parameters.description:
pickingby -  - Determines how the pick location is set.

 panel - Uses the  scoped in the  parameter. The uv position of the mouse on this component's control panel will be the uv position in the render that is sampled. The pick is active when the panel value specified by the  parameter is 1. parameters - Uses the U, V, and Pick parameters below for picking.

parameters.name: Panel
parameters.label: Panel
parameters.description: panel - Uses the  scoped in the  parameter. The uv position of the mouse on this component's control panel will be the uv position in the render that is sampled. The pick is active when the panel value specified by the  parameter is 1. parameters - Uses the U, V, and Pick parameters below for picking.

parameters.name: Panel Value
parameters.label: Panel Value
parameters.description: panelvalue - Specifies with panel value to use to trigger the pick when picking by panel.

parameters.name: U
parameters.label: U
parameters.description: picku - Sets the u coordinate when picking by parameters.

parameters.name: V
parameters.label: V
parameters.description: pickv - Sets the v coordinate when picking by parameters.

parameters.name: Select
parameters.label: Select
parameters.description: select - When picking by parameters, picking is active when this parameter = 1.

parameters.name: Activate Callbacks
parameters.label: Activate Callbacks
parameters.description: activatecallbacks - Enables   for each pick event.

parameters.name: Callbacks DAT
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: callbacks -  to a  containing callbacks for pick event received.

parameters.name: Fetch Position
parameters.label: Fetch Position
parameters.description:
position -  - Returns the position of the point picked on the geometry. Channels tx, ty, tz.

 no - Do not return position values. sopspace - Return position of point picked in  transform space. worldspace - Return position of point picked in world transform space. cameraspace - Return position of point picked in camera transform space. relativetoobj - Return position of point picked relative to object specified in   parameter.

parameters.name: No
parameters.label: No
parameters.description: no - Do not return position values. sopspace - Return position of point picked in  transform space. worldspace - Return position of point picked in world transform space. cameraspace - Return position of point picked in camera transform space. relativetoobj - Return position of point picked relative to object specified in   parameter.

parameters.name: Fetch Normal
parameters.label: Fetch Normal
parameters.description:
normal -  - Returns the normals of the point picked on the geometry. Channels nx, ny, nz.

 no - Do not return normal values. sopspace - Return normals of point picked in  transform space. worldspace - Return normals of point picked in world transform space. cameraspace - Return normals of point picked in camera transform space. relativetoobj - Return normals of point picked relative to object specified in   parameter.

parameters.name: Reference Object
parameters.label: Reference Object
parameters.description: referenceobj - Object used when fetching position or normals Relative to .

parameters.name: Fetch Point Color
parameters.label: Fetch Point Color
parameters.description: color - Returns the point color of the point picked on the geometry. Channels cr, cg, cb, ca.

parameters.name: Fetch Texture UV
parameters.label: Fetch Texture UV
parameters.description: uv - Returns the texture coordinates of the point picked on the geometry. Channels mapu, mapv, mapw.

parameters.name: Fetch Object Path
parameters.label: Fetch Object Path
parameters.description: path - Return the path to the object that is picked. This result requires and Info DAT with its Node  parameter referecning the Render Pick .

parameters.name: Fetch Depth
parameters.label: Fetch Depth
parameters.description: depth - Returns the depth of the point picked on the geometry. This value a non-linear ratio of the point's position between the near and far planes of the Depth Buffer. Channel is depth.

parameters.name: Fetch Instance ID
parameters.label: Fetch Instance ID
parameters.description: instanceid - Returns the  ID of the object. This will always be 0 if instancing is off. Channel is instance.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 1
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 1
parameters.description: customattrib1 - Specify which custom attributes to return from the object.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 1 Type
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 1 Type
parameters.description: customattrib1type - The type of attribute is selected from this menu.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 2
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 2
parameters.description: customattrib2 - Specify which custom attributes to return from the object.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 2 Type
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 2 Type
parameters.description: customattrib2type - The type of attribute is selected from this menu.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 3
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 3
parameters.description: customattrib3 - Specify which custom attributes to return from the object.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 3 Type
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 3 Type
parameters.description: customattrib3type - The type of attribute is selected from this menu.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 4
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 4
parameters.description: customattrib4 - Specify which custom attributes to return from the object.

parameters.name: Custom Attrib 4 Type
parameters.label: Custom Attrib 4 Type
parameters.description: customattrib4type - The type of attribute is selected from this menu.

parameters.name: Time Slice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: timeslice - Turning this on forces the channels to be "Time Sliced".  A  is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: Scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: scope - To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a  string on the Common page.

parameters.name: Sample Rate Match
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description:
srselect -  - Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Options are not available.

 first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.label: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.description: first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Export Method
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description:
exportmethod -  - This will determine how to connect the  channel to the parameter. Refer to the Export article for more information.

 datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: DAT Table by Index
parameters.label: DAT Table by Index
parameters.description: datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: Export Root
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: autoexportroot - This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by Channel Name is : are relative to.

parameters.name: Export Table
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: exporttable - The  used to hold the export information when using the  Table  Methods (See above).
